priority_reasoning: The committed structured-output run-set still shows `raw_v2` blocked across all three task groups on `holdout6-v2-non-overlap`.
The strengthened `--inject-hard-fields` track improved top-level hard-field preservation but remained blocked on `radishflow/suggest_flowsheet_edits` and still preserved one timeout.

The broader task-scoped builder batch is now 15/15 `reviewed_pass`, so the next unanswered route question is whether stronger raw output constraints can recover part of the v2 holdout before any sample expansion or model-size comparison.

blocked_components: The guided-decoding track now exists in scripts/run-radishmind-core-candidate.py, and the runtime can now execute it either through a native `GenerationConfig.guided_decoding` hook or through the local `custom_generate` scaffold-slot shim.

services/runtime/inference_provider.py and services/runtime/guided_decoding.py now define the provider-agnostic JSON schema request contract plus the local scaffold-slot shim, but the route still needs a same-boundary local batch result before any route decision update.
must_land_before_user_run: Keep the explicit candidate-wrapper CLI flag and mutual-exclusion boundary fixed before requesting any local model run.

Keep at least one deterministic repository check that fixes the flag name, summary policy, runtime-support failure boundary, and output-dir policy before any local model run is requested from the user.

planned_local_run.purpose: Rerun the non-overlap v2 holdout with schema-guided decoding before any sample expansion or model-size comparison.

planned_offline_eval.purpose: Read the guided-decoding candidate outputs without rerunning the model.

enter_when: schema_valid_rate improves beyond the committed raw_v2 baseline of 0.8333333333333334
task_valid_rate improves beyond the committed raw_v2 baseline of 0.4
timeout_count stays at 0 on the same 300 second policy
decision_branches.next_step: If guided decoding is clearly helpful, keep the same model and decide whether to expand this raw constrained route to full-holdout-9 before any `3B/4B` comparison.

enter_when: schema_valid_rate does not improve beyond 0.8333333333333334, or task_valid_rate does not improve beyond 0.4
radishflow/suggest_flowsheet_edits remains the blocked task group, or timeout_count is still non-zero
decision_branches.next_step: If guided decoding stays blocked on the same v2 slice, stop extending wrapper-level output-constraint work and move to `minimind-v` / `3B/4B` comparison rather than expanding sample count first.

operator_notes: The current local transformers runtime may satisfy this route through either a native guided-decoding hook or the custom_generate scaffold-slot shim; keep the run on the same machine and keep outputs under tmp/.
Keep this runbook on the same `holdout6-v2-non-overlap`, the same `300s` timeout, and the same raw boundary so the result is comparable to raw_v2 and hard-field injection.
Even if guided decoding improves machine metrics, it is still not raw promotion, training acceptance, or production contract acceptance without separate review and route-decision updates.
